title: centaurea pullata subsp. baetica
intro: Centaurea pullata subsp. baetica, also known as Baetic knapweed, is a perennial plant in the family Asteraceae, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy stems. Traditionally, Centaurea pullata subsp. baetica has been used in herbal medicine due to its purported medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
